comment: v2 — adds escalate_to_human and renames fetch_customer_v2_databricks to get_customer_metrics with USE-WHEN trigger.

tools.name: get_ticket
tools.description: Pull the full ticket record. Call this first.

tools.name: lookup_customer_by_email
tools.description: Resolve submitter email to customer_id and basic info.

tools.name: read_error_logs
tools.description: Read recent errors for a customer. Filterable by endpoint and status_code. since_hours defaults to 24.

tools.name: check_webhook_deliveries
tools.description: Webhook delivery log + retry status. USE WHEN ticket mentions webhooks not arriving or stopping.

tools.name: test_api_key
tools.description: Validate a key is active and check its scopes. USE WHEN ticket reports 401s or auth-related failures.

tools.name: inspect_integration_config
tools.description: Integration config inferred from recent traffic headers: SDK version, configured endpoints, retry settings.

tools.name: get_rate_limit_status
tools.description: Current usage vs limit, per endpoint, last hour.

tools.name: get_customer_metrics
tools.description: USE WHEN the ticket mentions rate limiting, usage anomalies, intermittent errors, slow performance, retry storms, unexplained 429s/500s. Returns per-endpoint volume + error rate + retry counts from the metrics warehouse. DO NOT answer rate-limit questions from plan config alone — call this first.

tools.name: escalate_to_human
tools.description: USE WHEN the issue requires a code change on our side, OR root cause is in our infrastructure (not the customer's), OR you cannot reproduce and the customer is blocked.
